quickFacts: Yes, it's expensive — budget €50-70/day minimum for food
Tap water is pristine and free from 1,200+ public fountains
Lake Zürich swimming is free — locals jump in from the Badi (public baths)
The ZVV day pass covers trains, trams, boats, and even funiculars

places.name: Lake Zürich
places.caption: Crystal-clear lake in the city centre
places.description: Swim at one of the historic Badi (lakeshore baths), take a boat cruise, or just stroll the promenade with gelato. Seebad Enge is the most scenic spot.

places.name: Old Town (Altstadt)
places.caption: Medieval lanes on both sides of the Limmat
places.description: Cobblestone streets, guild houses, and hidden courtyards. The Niederdorf side (east) is livelier with bars and restaurants, while the Lindenhof side has views.

places.name: Lindenhof
places.caption: Hilltop park with city views
places.description: A peaceful hilltop square above the old town where locals play giant chess. The view over the Limmat River and university is one of Zürich's best-kept secrets.

places.name: Kunsthaus Zürich
places.caption: World-class art museum
places.description: From Monet to Giacometti, Munch to Picasso. The new David Chipperfield extension is stunning. Free on Wednesdays — perfect for budget-conscious art lovers.

places.name: Zürich West (Kreis 5)
places.caption: Industrial area turned creative hub
places.description: Former factories now house trendy restaurants, clubs (Hive, Zukunft), and the Freitag flagship store in a tower of shipping containers. Viadukt market is under the arches.

places.name: Uetliberg
places.caption: Zürich's home mountain
places.description: A 20-minute train ride to panoramic views of the city, lake, and Alps. Hike the Planetenweg (Planet Trail) to Felsenegg or just enjoy the summit restaurant.

places.name: Grossmünster
places.caption: Romanesque church with twin towers
places.description: Zürich's most recognisable landmark. Climb the Karlsturm tower for bird's-eye views and check out the Giacometti stained glass windows and Sigmar Polke's vibrant panels.

places.name: Badi Culture
places.caption: Historic public baths on the river
places.description: Barfussbar, Frauenbadi, and Seebad Utoquai — these converted bathhouses are where locals swim, sunbathe, and drink. Some transform into bars and cinemas at night.

places.name: Bahnhofstrasse
places.caption: One of the world's most exclusive shopping streets
places.description: Window-shopping is free, and the street is beautifully car-free. Even if luxury isn't your thing, the Sprüngli bakery for Luxemburgerli macarons is unmissable.

places.name: Rhine Falls Day Trip
places.caption: Europe's largest waterfall, 45 min away
places.description: Take a boat right up to the thundering cascade and climb the rock in the middle. Combine with lunch in the medieval town of Schaffhausen.

places.name: Fraumünster Church
places.caption: Chagall stained glass
places.description: Romanesque church with five stunning Marc Chagall stained-glass windows (1970) and an Augusto Giacometti window.

places.name: Swiss National Museum
places.caption: Swiss history
places.description: Castle-like museum next to the main station covering Swiss cultural history from prehistory to present. New wing by Christ & Gantenbein.

places.name: Zürich West
places.caption: Industrial-chic quarter
places.description: Former industrial area now home to Freitag flagship store (shipping container tower), Im Viadukt market and rooftop bars.

places.name: Thermalbad & Spa
places.caption: Rooftop thermal pool
places.description: Brewery converted to thermal baths. Rooftop pool with city views, barrel saunas and mineral water from 600m underground.

foodScene: Zürich Geschnetzeltes (sliced veal in cream sauce) is the local classic. Fondue and raclette are available year-round at spots like Swiss Chuchi. Sprüngli Luxemburgerli are the city's signature macaron. Budget tip: supermarket deli counters (Coop, Migros) sell excellent ready-made meals for a fraction of restaurant prices.
